title: clarisia biflora
intro: Clarisia biflora, also known as the "Cinchona de la Selva," is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Clarisia biflora lie in its alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and prevent infections. Clarisia biflora can be prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a medicinal drink, or it can be used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
